# Blog Validation Checklist

Use this checklist during Phase 6 before delivering final content.

---

## Content Validation

- [ ] **Structure**: All required sections are present (introduce topic, explain why it matters, provide context, share core model/idea, add example/guidance, note boundaries/availability, call to action). Ensure that there are no restatements or redundant information.
- [ ] **Word count**: Verify length is ~1,140–1,285 words (enterprise target)
- [ ] **Next Steps**: Standalone posts include a Next Steps section
- [ ] **Word count**: ~1,140–1,285 words (standalone blog enterprise target)
- [ ] **Accuracy**: All technical information is correct and up to date
- [ ] **Completeness**: All sections from the approved outline are included
- [ ] **Flow**: Content reads logically with smooth transitions
- [ ] **Clarity**: Technical concepts are explained clearly for users new to the area

---

## Technical Validation

- [ ] **Links**: All documentation links are absolute URLs (`https://learn.microsoft.com/...`)
- [ ] **Link text**: Links use descriptive anchor text, not generic phrases (no "here", "docs", "click here")
- [ ] **Link settings**: Links exclude locale (no "en-us/") and are not internal/MSIT links
- [ ] **At least one doc link**: The post links to official documentation
- [ ] **Images**: Alt text is meaningful and descriptive (if images included)
- [ ] **GIFs**: Written descriptions are included in the blog body
- [ ] **Screenshots**: Clear, zoomed appropriately, with captions and alt text
- [ ] **Acronyms**: First use is spelled out, or acronym is widely known

---

## Style Validation

- [ ] **Tone**: Appropriate — educational, not marketing-driven
- [ ] **Marketing language**: No hype words present
- [ ] **Present tense**: Consistent use throughout
- [ ] **No pressure tactics**: No "Try it now!", "Don't miss out!", "Get started today!"
- [ ] **Terminology**: "item" not "artifact"; "SQL analytics endpoint" not "SQL endpoint"
- [ ] **Capitalization**: OneLake, SharePoint, Pipelines — correct throughout
- [ ] **No emojis**: Emojis are not accessible — remove any that appear
- [ ] **Input-agnostic verbs**: No "click" or "swipe" — use "select", "choose", "go to"
- [ ] **No "We're excited/pleased to announce"** in the opening
- [ ] **Title format**: Preview/GA status appended at end of title if applicable
- [ ] **Categories/Tags**: At least one category and 3–5 meaningful tags specified
